---
title: "nitrix.linalg.optimize"
generated: true
signature-hash: a40a7656c39f1195
description: "Small-residual nonlinear least squares: Gauss-Newton & Levenberg-Marquardt."
listing:
  id: nitrix-linalg-optimize-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-linalg-optimize/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub â€” fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.linalg.optimize`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC Â§6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Small-residual nonlinear least squares: Gauss-Newton & Levenberg-Marquardt.

Minimise $\tfrac{1}{2}\lVert r(x)\rVert^2$ for a residual map
$r: \mathbb{R}^P \to \mathbb{R}^M$.  The registration recipes are
the driving consumer ($r$ = warped-moving minus fixed, $x$ =
the transform's Lie parameters), but the optimiser is generic.

**Two Jacobian strategies, both GPU-native.**  Each step solves the
Gauss-Newton normal equations
$(J^{\top}J + \lambda I)\,\delta = -J^{\top}r$.

- `jacobian='assembled'` -- the `'auto'` default when the parameter
  count $P$ is small (the rigid/affine regime).  The
  $M \times P$ Jacobian is materialised in $P$ forward-mode
  passes (`jax.jacfwd`), the explicit $P \times P$ Gram matrix
  $J^{\top}J$ is formed, and the tiny SPD system is solved by
  [`cg`](nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd) on that explicit matrix.  Cheaper than the matrix-free matvec
  for small $P$: [`cg`](nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd) then costs $P \times P$ matmuls,
  not a full warp tangent + transpose per inner iteration.
- `jacobian='matrix_free'` -- for a large $P$.  The operator
  $v \mapsto J^{\top}(Jv)$ is assembled from a cached linearisation
  (`jax.linearize` for $Jv$, its `jax.linear_transpose` for
  $J^{\top}u$) -- so the $M \times P$ Jacobian is **never
  materialised** -- and the SPD system is solved with [`cg`](nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd) (matvecs
  only).

Both keep the hot loop on the GPU through the cuSolver wedge: no
factorisation, no host round-trip.

**The fixed-iteration loop is rolled with `lax.scan`** (not
Python-unrolled), so cold-compile time is roughly constant in `n_iters`
rather than linear -- one iteration is compiled, not `n_iters` copies.
It stays differentiable (`scan` is), so `jax.grad` through a
registration works out of the box.  [`implicit_least_squares`](nitrix-linalg-optimize/implicit_least_squares.qmd) adds the
implicit-function-theorem path: exact gradients at the optimum without
unrolling ($O(1)$ memory in the iteration count), the
differentiable-layer entry point for consumers like `entense`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-linalg-optimize-symbols}
:::
